hero.description: Helping businesses launch fast, modern, and scalable websites and platforms — from landing pages to complex React applications.
hero.card_title: An engineering approach to every task
hero.card_description: Combining strong visual style, thoughtful architecture, and practical development to ensure the project is beautiful, fast, and easy to scale.
hero.point_1: I design interface architecture to make the project easily scalable.
hero.point_2: I work remotely and flexibly, allowing me to manage projects effectively from anywhere.
hero.point_3: I create not just a beautiful page, but a clear and functional digital product.

features.section_title: An approach that saves businesses time and money
features.section_subtitle: I don't just build a pretty interface; I create a thoughtful solution that is easy to launch, maintain, and evolve.
features.card1_desc: I write clean, modular, and clear code so the project doesn't hit limits just a few months after launch.
features.card2_desc: I optimize interfaces, loading speeds, and project structure to ensure the site works fast and makes a strong first impression.
features.card3_desc: I create neat, intuitive, and visually strong interfaces that make product interaction simple and pleasant.
bottom_points: Clean development without chaos and overhead.
Focus on results, not just visuals.
An approach designed for future project growth.

seo_content.title: Frontend Developer for landing pages, business websites, and React platforms

items.title: E-commerce Frontend
items.text: User-friendly storefronts with fast catalog pages, smooth cart flows, and performance-first UI.

contact.title: Let's discuss your project
contact.subtitle: If you need a modern website, interface, or platform with a strong visual presentation and thoughtful architecture — write to me.
contact.placeholder_message: Briefly describe the project, task, or idea

pricing.section_subtitle: Below are the core areas I work with. Final cost and timeline depend on the structure, functionality, and complexity of the project.
pricing.card1_desc: Ideal for a personal brand, service, product, or a neat project presentation in a single strong screen or compact structure.
card1_points: A great option for launching without extra complexity.
pricing.card2_desc: For a business, service, or company that needs a wider structure, several pages, intuitive navigation, and scalability.
card2_points: Suitable for corporate and service websites.
More flexible structure and page logic.
A foundation for future project growth.
pricing.card3_desc: If you need an e-commerce store, a custom platform, or specific logic for a business task, the project is evaluated individually.
card3_points: Deadlines and cost depend on the scope of work.

faq.section_subtitle: I've gathered answers to the main questions that usually arise before we begin working together.
faq.side_note: If you have a non-standard task or no ready technical specification, that's not a problem — we can discuss everything and define the optimal format together.
faq.cta_text: Write to me directly, and I'll help you understand how to best implement your project.

items.question: What is included in the base price of the website?
items.answer: The base price includes a responsive website, one design option, minor revisions, and project deployment. Domain and hosting are not included — these costs remain with the client.

items.question: How many revisions are included?
items.answer: The base price includes 3 rounds of revisions. Each subsequent round is charged separately at 10€ per round.

items.question: What is required from the client to start?
items.answer: To start, it's helpful to prepare a logo, brand colors, text, website examples, references, and a rough understanding of the structure. It's best if this is collected in a PDF, but we can also discuss everything in a conversation.

items.question: Can I reach out without a ready specification?
items.answer: Yes, you can. If you don't have a ready spec yet, write to me — I'll help you understand the project structure, define the necessary pages, and set a basic direction for the start.

items.question: Can I order an urgent project?
items.answer: Yes, urgent projects are possible if I have availability. In such cases, the cost is discussed separately based on the urgency.

items.question: How does payment work?
items.answer: Payment is split into two parts: a 30% deposit before the start and the remaining balance after completion and deployment.

path_page.title: My Engineering Journey
path_page.subtitle: 6 years in development: from first experiments with code to building telemetry for industrial equipment and European logistics.
path_page.timeline_1_desc: First introduction to programming in 11th grade through a Java school project (JavaHamster). In 12th grade, started self-studying web technologies and fundamental languages: HTML/CSS/PHP and low-level C/C++/C#.
path_page.timeline_2_desc: Worked at Schiebel Antriebstechnik (Austria). Part of the mobile app dev team (C# MAUI) that connected in real-time to industrial actuators and displayed critical equipment parameters.
path_page.timeline_3_desc: Led the development of a Flutter app for monitoring boat VESC motors. The system collected real-time telemetry: speed, GPS, motor characteristics. Full hardware integration.
path_page.timeline_4_desc: Supporting and modernizing a large logistics ERP system operating in 6 European countries. Stack: C# .NET 4.7.2, SQL Server, WebForms, CI/CD. Legacy code optimization and migration to modern technologies.
path_page.timeline_5_desc: Started with a project for my father, then moved through friends and recommendations. Began with PHP but quickly switched to React for high-speed landing pages. Full cycle: from design to deployment.
path_page.timeline_6_desc: Moving towards complex projects: online stores, educational platforms, corporate sites. Specializing in React app architecture, performance optimization, and SEO.
path_page.skills_desc: Full cycle web development: from prototype to production systems

story_page.title: My Story
story_page.subtitle: How a school project led me to building industrial systems and independent web development.
story_page.section_1_text: In 2021, in the 11th grade, we did a school project in Java — it was my first real encounter with programming. By the 12th grade, I was self-studying web technologies and digging into C/C++/C#. That's when I realized I wanted to dedicate my life to development.
story_page.section_2_text: While working at McDonald's in Vienna, I found my first IT job in January 2023 at Schiebel Antriebstechnik. I joined the development team for a mobile app for industrial actuators — my first commercial project.
story_page.section_3_text: In May 2023, I led the development of a Flutter app for boat motors. The system collected real-time telemetry: speed, GPS, and motor characteristics. This was the first project where I was responsible for the full development cycle.
story_page.section_4_text: Since October 2023, I've been working on a large logistics system for 6 European countries. Here, I see how my solutions impact business in real-time. Simultaneously, I enrolled at FernUni Hagen to strengthen my academic foundation.
story_page.section_5_text: I started freelancing in December 2024. First for my father, then through recommendations. I switched from PHP to React because I realized: modern websites must be fast and scalable. Today, I build complex platforms and e-commerce solutions.
story_page.cta_text: Want to create a modern website, mobile app, or optimize logistics? Write to me — let's build it together.
